preamble: The transformation from a voiced palato-alveolar fricative to an alveolar flap is a type of rhotacism or lenition, where a sibilant weakens into a rhotic liquid, often occurring in intervocalic or coda positions.

examples.note: Historical shifts in certain dialects where palatal fricatives (often from older stops) were weakened into rhotics.
In some dialectal variations of Italian and Portuguese, palatal sounds have been reinterpreted as rhotic liquids in specific phonetic environments.

sources: Masica, C. P. (1991). The Indo-Aryan Languages.
Coutinho, I. L. (1976). Pontos de gramática histórica.
